words.def: located, done or used inside a building
words.example: an indoor swimming pool

words.def: inside or into a building
words.example: I prefer to stay indoors in this really hot weather.

words.def: the production of goods from raw materials, especially in factories
words.example: the needs of British industry

words.def: the effect that somebody/something has on the way a person thinks or behaves or on the way that something works or develops
words.example: His early work shows the influence of Cézanne and Matisse.

words.def: one of the things from which something is made, especially one of the foods that are used together to make a particular dish
words.example: Mix all the ingredients in a bowl.

words.def: to harm yourself or somebody else physically, especially in an accident
words.example: He injured his knee playing hockey.

words.def: harm done to a person’s or an animal’s body, for example in an accident
words.example: Two people sustained minor injuries.

words.def: not guilty of a crime, etc.; not having done something wrong
words.example: They have imprisoned an innocent man.

words.def: detailed information on how to do or use something
words.example: Follow the instructions on the packet carefully.

words.def: a person whose job is to teach somebody a practical skill or sport
words.example: a fitness/driving/ski instructor

words.def: the ability to learn, understand and think in a logical way about things; the ability to do this well
words.example: a person of high/average/low intelligence

words.def: to have a plan, result or purpose in your mind when you do something
words.example: We finished later than we had intended.

words.def: what you intend or plan to do; your aim
words.example: intention of doing something, I have no intention of going to the wedding.

words.def: the first part of a book or speech that gives a general idea of what is to follow
words.example: a book with an excellent introduction and notes

words.def: to buy property, shares in a company, etc. in the hope of making a profit
words.example: Now is a good time to invest.

words.def: a spoken or written request to somebody to do something or to go somewhere
words.example: to issue/extend an invitation

words.def: if a situation, an event or an activity involves something, that thing is an important or necessary part or result of it
words.example: involve something, Any investment involves an element of risk.

words.def: taking part in something; being part of something or connected with something
words.example: Some people tried to stop the fight but I didn't want to get involved.

words.def: a chemical element. Iron is a hard strong metal that is used to make steel and is also found in small quantities in blood and food.
words.example: iron gates/bars/railings

words.def: the study and use of electronic processes and equipment to store and send information of all kinds, including words, pictures and numbers (the abbreviation for ‘information technology’)
words.example: the increasing use of IT in all aspects of today's society

words.def: a type of music with strong rhythms, in which the players often improvise (= make up the music as they are playing), originally created by African American musicians at the beginning of the 20th century
words.example: traditional/modern jazz

words.def: a newspaper or magazine that deals with a particular subject or profession
words.example: a scientific/an academic journal

words.def: a person whose job is to collect and write news stories for newspapers, magazines, radio, television or online news sites
words.example: a freelance journalist

words.def: a person in a court who has the authority to decide how criminals should be punished or to make legal decisions
words.example: a High Court judge

words.def: most important; essential
words.example: the key issue/factor/point

words.def: the set of keys for operating a computer or typewriter, or the set of letters that you can touch to write on a smartphone or tablet
words.example: The program locks the keyboard until a password is given.

words.def: a movement with the foot or the leg, usually to hit something with the foot
words.example: the first kick of the game

words.def: an act of killing somebody deliberately
words.example: Their leader condemned the torture and killing of innocent civilians.

words.def: caring about others; gentle, friendly and generous
words.example: a very kind and helpful person

words.def: the act of kissing somebody/something
words.example: Come here and give me a kiss!

words.def: the sound of somebody hitting a door, window, etc. with their hand or with something hard to attract attention
words.example: knock on/at something, There was a knock at the door.

words.def: a laboratory
words.example: a computer/research lab

words.def: a room or building used for scientific research, experiments, testing, etc.
words.example: a clinical/research laboratory

words.def: the state of not having something or not having enough of something
words.example: lack of something, a lack of understanding/knowledge

words.def: the surface of the earth that is not sea
words.example: The new project will reclaim the land from the sea.

words.def: after anyone or anything else; at the end
words.example: He came last in the race.

words.def: the sound you make when you think that something is funny or silly
words.example: to give a laugh

words.def: the act or sound of laughing
words.example: to roar/howl with laughter

words.def: to put somebody/something in a particular position, especially when it is done gently or carefully
words.example: lay somebody/something + adv./prep., He laid a hand on my arm.

words.def: a quantity or sheet of something that lies over a surface or between surfaces
words.example: layer (of something), A thin layer of dust covered everything.

words.def: to go with or in front of a person or an animal to show the way or to make them go in the right direction
words.example: If you lead, I'll follow.

words.def: most important or most successful
words.example: a leading expert/authority/figure/member

words.def: a flat green part of a plant, growing from a stem or branch or from the root
words.example: lettuce/cabbage/oak leaves

words.def: to the smallest degree
words.example: He always turns up just when you least expect him.

words.def: material made by removing the hair or fur from animal skins and preserving the skins using special processes
words.example: a leather jacket

words.def: a talk that is given to a group of people to teach them about a particular subject, often as part of a university or college course
words.example: to go to/attend a lecture

words.def: connected with the law
words.example: the legal profession/system

words.def: time when you are not working or studying; free time
words.example: These days we have more money and more leisure to enjoy it.

words.def: the size or measurement of something from one end to the other
words.example: Measure the length of the line from A to B.

words.def: a statement made by somebody knowing that it is not true
words.example: to tell a lie

words.def: a machine that carries people or goods up and down to different levels in a building or a mine
words.example: It's on the sixth floor—let's take the lift.

words.def: the things that you like
words.example: We all have different likes and dislikes.

words.def: the greatest or smallest amount of something that is allowed
words.example: They imposed a strict spending limit.

words.def: a connection between two or more people or things
words.example: link between A and B, Police suspect there may be a link between the two murders.

words.def: either of the two soft edges at the opening to the mouth
words.example: The assistant pursed her lips.

words.def: in the form of a liquid; not a solid or a gas
words.example: liquid nitrogen

words.def: pieces of writing that are valued as works of art, especially novels, plays and poems (in contrast to technical books and newspapers, magazines, etc.)
words.example: English/American/French literature

words.def: living; not dead
words.example: live animals

words.def: alive now
words.example: all living things

words.def: belonging to or connected with the particular place or area that you are talking about or with the place where you live
words.example: local people/residents/businesses

words.def: to find the exact position of somebody/something
words.example: The mechanic located the fault immediately.

words.def: if something is located in a particular place, it exists there or has been put there
words.example: a small town located 30 miles south of Chicago

words.def: a place where something happens or exists; the position of something
words.example: a honeymoon in a secret location

words.def: unhappy because you have no friends or people to talk to
words.example: She lives alone and often feels lonely.

words.def: a large vehicle for carrying heavy loads by road
words.example: a lorry driver

words.def: the state of no longer having something or as much of something; the process that leads to this
words.example: I want to report the loss of a package.

words.def: making a lot of noise
words.example: loud laughter
